claim: Proposals shall be organized under wave-numbered folders in `proposals/` (`waveN-<slug>/`, optionally with batch subfolders), which serves the pending-vs-applied practical need without a dedicated `pending/`/`applied/` runtime split or a new graph node type; a proposal file lives in its wave folder from the TRANSLATE step through LAND.
why: Resolver verdict 2026-07-02 (verbatim): «да, нужно. наверно такие вещи нужно вести в отдельной папке» (English: 'yes, it's needed. probably such things should be tracked in a separate folder'). UPDATED 2026-07-13 (task #113, decision brief §9): reworded from 'shall live in a dedicated runtime folder structure (pending/ vs applied/), surfaced by the harness' to legalize the OBSERVED practice — proposals live in wave-numbered folders under proposals/ (waveN-<slug>/, optionally with batch subfolders), which already serves the pending/applied practical need without a formal pre-approval staging step or a new graph node type. Verified today (2026-07-13): proposals/ carries 13 wave-named folders spanning wave1 through wave9 (9 wave numbers, several multi-folder: wave1×2, wave3×3, wave9×2), plus 2 task*-named folders = 15 entries total; wave6 alone landed 8 sub-batches in one session. The wave-folder practice models the 'applied' half well; the 'pending' half is handled in-workflow (TRANSLATE drafts a .json, PRESENT shows the resolver, LAND applies) without needing an on-disk pre-approval artifact. The literal pending/applied split (option (a)) would require a workflow change — changing WHEN a proposal file gets written (before vs after approval) — which is more invasive than the engineering task alone (decision brief §9). The P6/PENDING_PROPOSAL label in cmd/hotam/what_now.go priorityLabel remains dead code (no signal producer); no .runtime/ proposal tracking exists. (Historical Python landing split spec/.runtime/proposals/ into pending/ vs applied/, moved by apply_proposal.py — not carried into the Go port.) PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists.
